nodes.text: Immortal animal spirit dancing on cave wall — I cannot grasp but merely perceive some primal truth into this quest, inserted dis-honestly.
Faust stands up and inspects himself in the mirror.
My world of possibility expands with each new breath. They give this drug to shut-ins — agoraphfolk struggling with writer's block.
Faust looks at his phone.
Time to make some work. Dime-this corporate skrill. All the tech-pharm nerds — holla at me, please.
Faust exits the office, emerges into the drugstore proper, looks out at the slow medicated trickle of society.
From my whitewalled fortress of solitude I emerge and bask upon my kingdom — of cheap fluorescents and impulse buys. For I rule it well.
Deborah is helping a customer at the front table. Faust saunters up.
Everything going great today.
Bit early to tell.
Some days I feel like an IV drip.
Pretty much sums up my view of purgatory.
A customer interrupts — a happy old woman.
When most the ones my age learn how to be happy, they've already lost so much time. So enjoy it. Be young and happy forever.
Faust checks the bottle and hands it over to her.
Another happy customer served.
Another one who sees the light oncoming.
I feel like Charon ferrying.
Aptly put.
Faust's other assistant, Eric, enters.
Yo yo ma in the hizzouse.
What he got in that grill?
Just a bridge and two f-holes.
MC R-I-B is back!
Faust and Eric palm-shake. Eric puts down lunch.
I smell me some Vietnamese.
Deborah is already digging into hers.
So good. Give it over.
Six eggrolls between the three of us.
Hint: lunch is on me — because I want three.
